task.objective: Replace the old scan-submit-review flow with a translator-style client-side masking workspace.

requirements.item: Raw input remains on the left and protected output remains on the right.
A short suspension spinner appears while masking is rebuilt locally.
Country selection is modal-based, multi-select, and browser-locale aware.
Settings are modal-based and expose global-only detection.
Group controls and per-match controls are unified into a single inline form.
Flag emojis are used for country selection labels.
Mixed-language scope selections trigger a warning.
Copy actions always target the protected output only.

engineeringConstraints.item: Keep masking logic deterministic inside the masking engine.
Keep browser locale detection and storage in state utilities.
Keep constants, declarations, and reusable helpers in their dedicated folders.
Use SCSS mixins, functions, container queries, media queries, pseudo selectors, and progressive enhancement primitives.
Back new rules and scope logic with Jest fixtures and Playwright coverage.
